rows.agent_rationale: Rank-1 condition-specific regulator with a 3014-gene Stim8hr footprint under on-target KD and near-inert K562 behavior (1 DE, cell-type-specific).
rows.specificity: Rest 175 DE, K562 1 DE, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
Loss of the stimulated DE footprint after orthogonal knockdown would lower priority.

rows.agent_rationale: On-target KD drives 1167 DE at Stim48hr against only 58 at Rest and 5 in K562, a clean cell-type-specific stimulated footprint.
rows.specificity: Rest 58 DE, K562 5 DE, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
Loss of the stimulated DE footprint after orthogonal knockdown would lower priority.

rows.agent_rationale: Despite 780 on-target Stim48hr DE, the Rest condition is flagged as putative off-target and it shows the set's highest K562 DE (20), a specificity concern.
rows.specificity: Rest 113 DE, K562 20 DE, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
Loss of the stimulated DE footprint after orthogonal knockdown would lower priority.

rows.agent_rationale: On-target KD with 720 Stim8hr DE and inert K562 (1 DE), but the lowest score and relatively high Rest activity (190 DE) make it a lower-priority advance.
rows.specificity: Rest 190 DE, K562 1 DE, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
Loss of the stimulated DE footprint after orthogonal knockdown would lower priority.

rows.agent_rationale: Top-scoring candidate (1053, rank 5) with the largest stimulated footprint (619 DE at Stim48hr under on-target KD) that is cell-type-specific (inert in K562), making it the strongest proposal-only lead to advance.
rows.specificity: Rest 116 DE, K562 13 DE, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
Loss of the stimulated DE footprint after orthogonal knockdown would lower priority.

rows.agent_rationale: Solid stim-specific footprint (457 DE at Stim48hr on-target KD, only 20 at Rest) but the Stim8hr arm showed no on-target KD, so it is worth advancing contingent on capacity.
rows.specificity: Rest 20 DE, K562 1 DE, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
Loss of the stimulated DE footprint after orthogonal knockdown would lower priority.

rows.agent_rationale: Its footprint is confined to Stim8hr (407 DE) and collapses to 2 DE by Stim48hr, indicating a transient signal that warrants holding as a ranked backup rather than immediate advancement.
rows.specificity: Rest 19 DE, K562 1 DE, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
Loss of the stimulated DE footprint after orthogonal knockdown would lower priority.

rows.agent_rationale: Cleanest stim-specific signal (389 DE at Stim48hr on-target KD versus only 11 at Rest, inert in K562) but lowest score/rank (928, rank 8), so advance only if capacity permits.
rows.specificity: Rest 11 DE, K562 13 DE, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
Loss of the stimulated DE footprint after orthogonal knockdown would lower priority.

rows.agent_rationale: Rank-9 with an on-target KD Stim48hr footprint (386 DE), but cross-cell-type is not_tested so its non-immune specificity gap is unresolved.
rows.specificity: Rest 18 DE, K562 not measured, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
A broad non-immune effect in a follow-up transfer assay would lower priority.
broad non-immune effect appears in a transfer assay

rows.agent_rationale: Its 350-DE signal is confined to the single Stim48hr on-target condition while Stim8hr/Rest show no on-target KD, and transfer is not_tested, making it the weaker untested candidate.
rows.specificity: Rest 11 DE, K562 not measured, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
A broad non-immune effect in a follow-up transfer assay would lower priority.
broad non-immune effect appears in a transfer assay

rows.agent_rationale: K562-inert cell-type specificity is confirmed and strongest Stim8hr is on-target, but the Stim48hr putative-off-target flag and elevated Rest DE (53) warrant caution before assay design.
rows.specificity: Rest 53 DE, K562 0 DE, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
Loss of the stimulated DE footprint after orthogonal knockdown would lower priority.

rows.agent_rationale: On-target KD at the strongest condition (Stim48hr, 263 DE) with low Rest DE and confirmed K562-inert cell-type specificity leaves only the standard orthogonal-reproduction risk.
rows.specificity: Rest 17 DE, K562 0 DE, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
Loss of the stimulated DE footprint after orthogonal knockdown would lower priority.

rows.agent_rationale: Strongest-condition footprint (Stim48hr, 250 DE) is under confirmed on-target KD with low Rest (11 DE) and K562-inert cell-type-specific behavior.
rows.specificity: Rest 11 DE, K562 1 DE, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
Loss of the stimulated DE footprint after orthogonal knockdown would lower priority.

rows.agent_rationale: On-target Stim8hr signal (496 DE) is strong but the high Rest DE (261) derives from a no-on-target-KD condition and cross-cell transfer is not tested.
rows.specificity: Rest 261 DE, K562 not measured, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
A broad non-immune effect in a follow-up transfer assay would lower priority.
Rest effect grows on replicate
broad non-immune effect appears in a transfer assay

rows.agent_rationale: On-target KD footprint reproduces across two stim conditions (329 and 181 DE) with K562-inert specificity, though Rest DE (110) sits under a non-KD condition.
rows.specificity: Rest 110 DE, K562 0 DE, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
Loss of the stimulated DE footprint after orthogonal knockdown would lower priority.

rows.agent_rationale: Stim8hr is flagged putative off-target and cross-cell is not tested, weakening its lowest-ranked (score 706) stimulated on-target signal.
rows.specificity: Rest 104 DE, K562 not measured, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
A broad non-immune effect in a follow-up transfer assay would lower priority.
broad non-immune effect appears in a transfer assay

rows.agent_rationale: On-target KD drives a 374-DE Stim8hr footprint that is inert in K562 (1 DE, cell-type-specific), and its 291-DE Rest signal has no on-target KD so the activation-specific case holds.
rows.specificity: Rest 291 DE, K562 1 DE, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
A larger Rest effect on replicate would make this look less activation-specific.
Rest effect grows on replicate

rows.agent_rationale: On-target KD confirms only a modest stim window (261 vs 181 Rest DE) while the large 796-DE Stim48hr footprint lacks on-target KD, and its flagged risk is needing orthogonal reproduction.
rows.specificity: Rest 181 DE, K562 5 DE, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
Loss of the stimulated DE footprint after orthogonal knockdown would lower priority.

rows.agent_rationale: It is a known CollecTRI TF with 59 targets, the largest on-target stim footprint (567 DE), and the cleanest cross-cell-type profile (0 K562 DE).
rows.specificity: Rest 325 DE, K562 0 DE, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
A larger Rest effect on replicate would make this look less activation-specific.
Rest effect grows on replicate

rows.agent_rationale: Lowest score with on-target-KD-confirmed Rest DE (308) nearly matching Stim48hr (422), making it the least activation-specific, and its 15-target regulon is a weaker anchor than IRF4.
rows.specificity: Rest 308 DE, K562 2 DE, RPE1 not measured
stop_rules: failed on-target knockdown in the stimulated condition
A larger Rest effect on replicate would make this look less activation-specific.
Rest effect grows on replicate
